paragraphs: A state-level public service system in Colorado for people with intellectual and developmental disabilities. Funding supports programs and service networks, including community-centered boards that coordinate local access.

citationBlocks.label: Formal framework
citationBlocks.afterLinksNote: See Article 10 in the PDF (search for "25.5-10"). If that file is replaced, open the latest Title 25.5 PDF from OLLS.

links.text: Individuals with Intellectual and Developmental Disabilities (HCPF)

paragraphs: A {{term:term-special-districts|special district}} local government that operates public library services in its legal service area, including branches, collections, and community programs. Some cities in the county run separate city library systems; this levy is for the district shown above, not those city systems.

citationBlocks.label: Formal framework
citationBlocks.afterLinksNote: Library districts and public libraries are in Title 24. Open the latest Title 24 PDF from OLLS if the year changes.

links.text: Who we are (funding and service area)

paragraphs: A multi-county {{term:term-special-districts|special district}} local government that plans and operates regional transit (bus, rail, and related services) in its service area. Property tax is one funding source; the district also uses other revenue such as sales tax.

citationBlocks.label: Formal framework
citationBlocks.afterLinksNote: Regional transportation districts are organized under Title 32. If the PDF is replaced, open the latest Title 32 from OLLS and search for "Regional Transportation" or "32-9".
